rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use visible identifiers and named constants in type-level contracts.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponents.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be syntactically valid and scoped to their owning component.
Equation and constant declarations in AGREE should match the types of referenced expressions.
Avoid using AADL property names directly as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
Assignments in AGREE should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees follow the pattern `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;`.
Equations and constants are declared with `eq name : type = expression;` and `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing properties
Implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
